zh.lifeSummary: 當Samuel Bliss於1624年出生在英國戴文貝爾斯同時，他的父親Thomas Bliss41歲，母親Margaret Hulins29歲。他於1665年11月10日與Mary Leonard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他去世於1720年3月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德享年96歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德斯普林菲爾德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Samuel Bliss于1624年出生在英国戴文貝爾斯同时，他的父亲Thomas Bliss41岁，母亲Margaret Hulins29岁。他于1665年11月10日与Mary Leonard在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和9个女儿的父母。他去世于1720年3月23日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德享年96岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德斯普林菲爾德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Bliss
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a cheerful person, from Middle English blisse ‘joy’. Compare Blissett .

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from the village of Blay in Calvados, France, recorded in 1077 in the form Bleis. The village of Stoke Bliss in Worcestershire takes the second part of its name from a Norman family de Blez, recorded several times in the county from the 13th century.
German: nickname for a cheerful person, from Middle High German blīde ‘happy, friendly’. It is also found in France.

variants.name: Blish
meanings: English: rare variant of   Bliss  . The surname seems to have died out in Britain.

variants.name: Blissett
meanings: from the Middle English female personal name Blisset or Blissot, apparently a pet form of Middle English bliss ‘gladness, joy’ used as a personal name.
variant of Blessed, from Middle English iblescede ‘blessed, happy, fortunate’. Compare Bleazard .

variants.name: Blitsch
meanings: German: variant of Bliss 3. The surname Blitsch is rare in Germany. Compare Blitch .

nameMeanings.name: Samuel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (Hebrew Shemuel), possibly meaning ‘He (God) has hearkened’ (presumably to the prayers of a mother for a son). It may also be understood as a contracted form of Hebrew sha'ulme'el meaning ‘asked of God’. In the case of Samuel the son of Hannah, this would be more in keeping with his mother's statement ‘Because I have asked him of the Lord’ (1 Samuel 1:20). Living in the 11th century bc , Samuel was a Hebrew judge and prophet of the greatest historical importance, who established the Hebrew monarchy, anointing as king both Saul and, later, David. In the Authorized Version two books of the Old Testament are named after him, although in Roman Catholic and Orthodox versions of the Bible they are known as the first and second Book of Kings. The story of Samuel being called by God while still a child serving in the house of Eli the priest (1 Samuel 3) is of great vividness and has moved countless generations. In England and America the name was particularly popular among the 16th-century Puritans and among Nonconformists from the 17th to the 19th century. It became fashionable again in the 1990s.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

historicEvents.title: South Sea Bubble
historicEvents.description: The South Sea Bubble Bill was passed by the House of Lords in 1720. This allowed the South Sea company to monopolize trade with South America. The company underwrote the English National Debt which promised 5% interest from the government. As shares rose exponentially, many companies were created and many fortunes were made. The stocks crashed and many people lost their money which caused them to become destitute overnight and suicide was common.  Robert Walpole took charge of the South Sea Bubble Financial Crisis by dividing the national debt between the Bank of England, the Treasury, and the Sinking Fund.

en.lifeSummary: When Samuel Bliss was born in 1624, in Belstone, Devon, England, his father, Thomas Bliss, was 41 and his mother, Margaret Hulins, was 29. He married Mary Leonard on 10 November 1665, in Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 9 daughters. He died on 23 March 1720, in Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 96, and was buried in Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

Il a épousé Mary Leonard le 10 novembre 1665, à Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 9 filles. Il est décédé le 23 mars 1720, à Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 96 ans, et a été enterré à Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

Lui sposato Mary Leonard il 10 novembre 1665, nel Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 9 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Samuel Blissが1624年にイギリスデボンベルストーンで生まれた時，父Thomas Blissは41歳，母Margaret Hulinsは29歳であった。1665年11月10日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアスプリングフィールドでMary Leonardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1720年3月23日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアスプリングフィールドで96歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアスプリングフィールドスプリングフィールド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Ba: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드
ko.St Nicholas' Church, Gloucester, Glouces: 영국 그루스스터실르 그루스스터 스트 닉홀아스' 처르치
ko.Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hamps: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드 스프링필드 세멧리
ko.lifeSummary: Samuel Bliss(이)가 1624년 영국 데폰 벨스톤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Bliss(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Hulins(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1665년 11월 10일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드에서 Mary Leonard(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1720년 3월 23일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드에서 96세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드 스프링필드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Samuel Bliss nasceu em 1624, em Belstone, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Bliss, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Hulins, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Leonard em 10 de novembro de 1665, em Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 9 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de março de 1720, em Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 96 anos, e foi sepultado em Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Samuel Bliss родился в 1624 г. в местности белстоун, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Bliss было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Hulins, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Leonard 10 ноября 1665 г. в местности спрингфиелд, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 9 дочерей. Он умер 23 марта 1720 г. в местности спрингфиелд, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 96 лет и был похоронен в местности спрингфиелд кеметири, спрингфиелд, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Samuel Bliss nació en 1624, en Belstone, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Bliss, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Margaret Hulins, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Mary Leonard el 10 de noviembre de 1665, en Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 23 de marzo de 1720, en Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 96 años, y fue sepultado en Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
